title: Extension manifest
description: Formal schema for the framework-core extension manifest contract per D29 (extension manifest contract + validation flow). Notation: JSON Schema Draft 2020-12 per D28. Layer-3 formal schema per D11 — constrains the structure of the parsed manifest, not its wire format (instances may be serialized as JSON, YAML, etc. and round-trip through this schema). Cross-kind referential integrity (e.g., spec-ref resolution, identifier collision across extensions, conformance of referenced specs) is out of scope per D28 and is handled by the framework conformance validator (workstream 4).

id.description: Stable extension identifier (D29 §1 Identity). Identifiers registered by an extension are implicitly namespaced by this id; canonical fully-qualified form is '<extension-id>:<identifier>'.

version.description: Version designator for this extension (D29 §1). Semver-shaped at layer-3 formal schema; range-comparable.

vocabulary-registrations.description: List of (slot, identifier, spec-ref) tuples that add values to a core open-vocabulary slot (D29 §2). Required slot; may be [] (per D13 required-with-explicit-empty pattern).

provisions.description: List of (kind, id, spec-ref) tuples — actual instances of kinds that this extension provides (D29 §3). Kinds that admit provisions: substrate, shape, adapter, specialist. (workspace, event, actor, work-unit are not extension-provided per D29.) Required slot; may be [] (per D13 required-with-explicit-empty pattern).

dependencies.description: Dependencies the extension's provisions need to compose against (D29 §4). Both sub-slots are required; both may be [] (per D13 required-with-explicit-empty pattern).

required-core-capabilities.description: Abstract core capabilities the provisions need (D17: hooks, skills, event-streaming). Required, may be [].

required-extensions.description: Other extensions this one depends on, by (id, version-range). Required, may be []. Circular-dep detection + resolution = workstream 4.

extension-id.description: Lowercase kebab-case identifier; leading letter; no ':' (reserved as namespace separator in canonical fully-qualified form '<extension-id>:<identifier>' per D29 'Identifier namespacing').

local-identifier.description: Bare identifier within an extension's own manifest. Same charset as extension-id; namespacing is implicit per D29.

semver.description: Semver 2.0.0-shaped MAJOR.MINOR.PATCH with optional pre-release and build metadata (per D29: 'semver-shaped at layer-3 formal schema').

version-range.description: npm-style version range expression. Admits operators ^, ~, >=, >, <=, <, =, or a bare version. Multi-clause ranges (e.g., '>=1.0.0 <2.0.0') are admitted; precise grammar is loader-implementation territory per D11 — schema enforces shape, validator enforces resolution.

spec-ref.description: Reference to a value's specification (typically a JSON Schema per D28; for some slots, additionally a behavioral contract document). Treated as opaque-string-resolvable-by-the-loader per D29 Tensions — within-package paths, URLs, and registry references are all admissible at this layer; resolution semantics are implementation per D11.

extension-reference.description: Reference to another extension by id + version range (used by 'extends' and by 'dependencies.required-extensions[]').

vocabulary-slot.description: The set of framework-core open-vocabulary slots into which an extension may register a value, per D29 §2. Closed enum at this schema layer; if framework-core gains a new open-vocabulary slot, this enum is updated as part of the kind-contract change.

vocabulary-registration.description: A single (slot, identifier, spec-ref) tuple registering one value into one core open-vocabulary slot. The identifier is namespaced to the declaring extension per D29 'Identifier namespacing'.

provision-kind.description: The set of kinds that admit extension-provided provisions, per D29 §3. Excludes workspace (per-deployment manifest) and event / actor / work-unit (runtime instances).

provision.description: A single (kind, id, spec-ref) tuple — one provision the extension provides. The provision conforms to its kind's layer-3 formal schema (per-kind schemas are workstream 3).
